program.name: ANZ Graduate Programs
program.duration_text: You’ll get 18 or 24 months of critical experiences across our business, including exposure to a diverse range of people, environments and situations.
program.rotation_text: Our six-monthly rotations are the best way to learn about the bank and your options for the role you’d like to move into at the end of the program.

eligibility.work_rights: You should be an Australian or New Zealand citizen, or hold permanent residency in the program country you are applying for.
eligibility.graduation_window: You should be in your final year of university studies (bachelor degree or higher), or have graduated in the last three years.
minimum_requirements: You should be an Australian or New Zealand citizen, or hold permanent residency in the program country you are applying for.
You should be in your final year of university studies (bachelor degree or higher), or have graduated in the last three years.

stages.name: Digital interview and game-based assessment
stages.details: A bunch of games that help ANZ get to know you better.

stages.name: Personality questionnaire
stages.details: Some questions about how you like to work.

stages.name: Interview preparation
stages.details: Get insider tips from past grads on how to do well in the interview.

stages.name: Face to face interview
stages.details: Meet some ANZ leaders so they can learn more about you and you can get to know ANZ.

tips: You do not need to submit a CV or cover letter for the application form.

exec_themes: Customer-first focus (improving digital and human service interactions)
Simplification and productivity (reducing duplication, divesting non-core assets, lowering costs)
Resilience via stronger non-financial risk management (trust, safety, accountability; APRA remediation)
Delivering value through higher-returning growth and disciplined capital management
Integration of Suncorp Bank to capture a ‘step change’ in earnings and deliver value
Accelerating ANZ Plus as the group’s digital front-end
Maintaining strong credit quality through cautious customer selection and de-risking
commercial_context.profit_engine: ANZ describes its profit engine as a diversified banking and markets group that grows earnings through (1) core banking income (lending and deposits) across Australia, New Zealand and other geographies, (2) Markets and capital markets performance (including customer activity in Markets and debt capital markets), and (3) disciplined cost/productivity programs, all supported by strong credit quality and capital strength. The inclusion and integration of Suncorp Bank is also described as a material earnings contributor.
commercial_context.headwinds: ANZ highlights a challenging and volatile external environment, including heightened geopolitical disruption (including US trade policy changes) and global supply chain disruption creating uncertainty. It also flags the need to remediate and lift capability in non-financial risk management under regulatory scrutiny (including an APRA enforceable undertaking), and the execution challenge of large change programs (e.g., integrating Suncorp Bank and scaling the ANZ Plus digital front-end).
commercial_context.esg: ANZ positions ESG largely around supporting customers’ transition (including net-zero transition) through financing and sustainable finance products (e.g., green/social/sustainability/sustainability-linked loans, bonds and trade products). ANZ also reports activity in sustainable finance deals and frames ESG as part of its stakeholder outcomes (alongside broader climate-related disclosures in investor materials).
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Recent strategy emphasis is framed around four strategic pillars (Customer first, Simplicity, Resilience, Delivering value) and five near-term priorities under new leadership: cultural reset/leadership embed, faster Suncorp Bank integration, accelerating ANZ Plus delivery, reducing duplication/simplifying the organisation (including role reductions), and strengthening non-financial risk management (including APRA-approved remediation planning).

notes: The main Graduate Programs page lists program structure, streams, locations, and process steps, but it does not clearly state application open/close dates on the page itself.
Application open/close dates were taken from an ANZ Newsroom article about the 2026 program (Australia and New Zealand).

sources.title: From graduates to leaders: Igniting your passion | ANZ

notes: Eligibility wording is stated directly under “Am I eligible?” on the Graduate Programs page.
The Graduate Programs page lists the main application stages.
The tip about not needing a CV or cover letter is stated in an ANZ media release, and is consistent with the “Quick online application” stage wording on the Graduate Programs page.

sources.title: ANZ awarded most Popular Banking, Finance and Insurance Employer at Top100 Graduate Employers Awards 2025 | ANZ

notes: Some ESG details in the 2024 ESG investor pack could not be fully extracted from text due to PDF rendering/availability limits in this run; only clearly readable elements were used.

The 2025 Strategy Update investor discussion pack PDF was not text-readable in this run (likely scanned/secured), so themes were taken from the 2025 Full Year Results investor discussion pack pages shown and from 2025 Half Year transcript/speaking notes.
Where ANZ uses broad strategic language (e.g., ‘delivering value’), the summary translates it into plain English without adding numbers or targets beyond what is explicitly shown.
